name: litestar-users

metadata.name: litestar-users
metadata.summary: Authentication and user management for Litestar

documentation:
# litestar-users

Effortlessly add secure authentication to your [Litestar](https://litestar.dev/) application.

## Features

- Session, JWT and JWTCookie authentication backends
- Customizable pre- and post-operation hooks
- Optional RBAC (Role based access control)
- Pre-configured route handlers for:
  - Authentication
  - Registration and verification
  - Password recovery
  - Administrative user and role management

## Getting started

### Installation

`pip install litestar-users`

### Documentation

[Read the documentation](https://lonelyvikingmichael.github.io/litestar-users/)

Otherwise [check out the examples](https://github.com/LonelyVikingMichael/litestar-users/tree/main/examples)

documentation: Get the LitestarUsersPlugin from the Litestar application.

functions.name: litestar_users.utils.get_sqlalchemy_plugin
documentation: Get the SQLAlchemyPlugin from the Litestar application.

functions.name: litestar_users.utils.async_session
documentation: Get an async session outside of a Litestar request context.

functions.name: litestar_users.utils.get_user_service
documentation: Get a `UserService` instance outside of a Litestar request context.

classes.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.__init__
documentation:
User service constructor.

Args:
    secret: Secret string for securely signing tokens.
    user_auth_identifier: The `User` model attribute to identify the user during authorization.
    user_repository: A `UserRepository` instance.
    hash_schemes: Schemes to use for password encryption.
    role_repository: A `RoleRepository` instance.
    require_verification_on_registration: Whether the registration of a new user requires verification.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.add_user
documentation:
Create a new user programmatically.

Args:
    user: User model instance.
    verify: Set the user's verification status to this value.
    activate: Set the user's active status to this value.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.register
documentation:
Register a new user and optionally run custom business logic.

Args:
    data: User creation data transfer object.
    request: The litestar request that initiated the action.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.get_user
documentation:
Retrieve a user from the database by id.

Args:
    id_: UUID corresponding to a user primary key.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.get_user_by
documentation:
Retrieve a user from the database by arbitrary keyword arguments.

Args:
    **kwargs: Keyword arguments to pass as filters.

Examples:
    ```python
    service = UserService(...)
    john = await service.get_one(email="john@example.com")
    ```

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.update_user
documentation:
Update arbitrary user attributes in the database.

Args:
    data: User update data transfer object.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.delete_user
documentation:
Delete a user from the database.

Args:
    id_: UUID corresponding to a user primary key.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.authenticate
documentation:
Authenticate a user.

Args:
    data: User authentication data transfer object.
    request: The litestar request that initiated the action.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.generate_token
documentation:
Generate a limited time valid JWT.

Args:
    user_id: ID of the user to provide the token to.
    aud: Context of the token

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.initiate_verification
documentation:
Initiate the user verification flow.

Args:
    user: The user requesting verification.

Notes:
    - The user verification flow is not initiated when `require_verification_on_registration` is set to `False`.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.send_verification_token
documentation:
Execute custom logic to send the verification token to the relevant user.

Args:
    user: The user requesting verification.
    token: An encoded JWT bound to verification.

Notes:
- Develepors need to override this method to facilitate sending the token via email, sms etc.
- This method is not invoked when `require_verification_on_registration` is set to `False`.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.verify
documentation:
Verify a user with the given JWT.

Args:
    encoded_token: An encoded JWT bound to verification.
    request: The litestar request that initiated the action.

Raises:
    InvalidTokenException: If the token is expired or tampered with.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.initiate_password_reset
documentation:
Initiate the password reset flow.

Args:
    email: Email of the user who has forgotten their password.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.send_password_reset_token
documentation:
Execute custom logic to send the password reset token to the relevant user.

Args:
    user: The user requesting the password reset.
    token: An encoded JWT bound to the password reset flow.

Notes:
- Develepors need to override this method to facilitate sending the token via email, sms etc.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.reset_password
documentation:
Reset a user's password given a valid JWT.

Args:
    encoded_token: An encoded JWT bound to the password reset flow.
    password: The new password to hash and store.

Raises:
    InvalidTokenException: If the token has expired or been tampered with.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.pre_login_hook
documentation:
Execute custom logic to run custom business logic prior to authenticating a user.

Useful for authentication checks against external sources,
eg. current membership validity or blacklists, etc
Must return `False` or raise a custom exception to cancel authentication.

Args:
    data: Authentication data transfer object.
    request: The litestar request that initiated the action.

Notes:
    Uncaught exceptions in this method will break the authentication process.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.post_login_hook
documentation:
Execute custom logic to run custom business logic after authenticating a user.

Useful for eg. updating a login counter, updating last known user IP
address, etc.

Args:
    user: The user who has authenticated.
    request: The litestar request that initiated the action.

Notes:
    Uncaught exceptions in this method will break the authentication process.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.pre_registration_hook
documentation:
Execute custom logic to run custom business logic prior to registering a user.

Useful for authorization checks against external sources,
eg. membership API or blacklists, etc.

Args:
    data: User creation data transfer object
    request: The litestar request that initiated the action.

Notes:
- Uncaught exceptions in this method will result in failed registration attempts.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.post_registration_hook
documentation:
Execute custom logic to run custom business logic after registering a user.

Useful for updating external datasets, sending welcome messages etc.

Args:
    user: User ORM instance.
    request: The litestar request that initiated the action.

Notes:
- Uncaught exceptions in this method could result in returning a HTTP 500 status
code while successfully creating the user in the database.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.post_verification_hook
documentation:
Execute custom logic to run custom business logic after a user has verified details.

Useful for eg. updating sales lead data, etc.

Args:
    user: User ORM instance.
    request: The litestar request that initiated the action.

Notes:
- Uncaught exceptions in this method could result in returning a HTTP 500 status
code while successfully validating the user.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.get_role
documentation:
Retrieve a role by id.

Args:
    id_: ID of the role.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.get_role_by_name

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.add_role

Add a new role to the database.

Args:
    data: A role creation data transfer object.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.update_role
documentation:
Update a role in the database.

Args:
    id_: UUID corresponding to the role primary key.
    data: A role update data transfer object.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.delete_role
documentation:
Delete a role from the database.

Args:
    id_: UUID corresponding to the role primary key.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.assign_role
documentation:
Add a role to a user.

Args:
    user_id: ID of the user to receive the role.
    role_id: ID of the role to add to the user.

methods.name: litestar_users.service.BaseUserService.revoke_role
documentation:
Revoke a role from a user.

Args:
    user_id: ID of the user to revoke the role from.
    role_id: ID of the role to revoke.

classes.name: litestar_users.config.UserManagementHandlerConfig

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.UserManagementHandlerConfig.path_prefix
documentation:
The prefix for the router path.

By default, the path will be suffixed with `'/{user_id:<type>}'`.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.UserManagementHandlerConfig.guards
documentation: A list of callable [Guards][litestar.types.Guard] that determines who is authorized to manage other users.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.UserManagementHandlerConfig.opt
documentation: Optional route handler [opts][litestar.controller.Controller.opt] to provide additional context to Guards.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.UserManagementHandlerConfig.tags
documentation: A list of string tags to append to the schema of the route handler(s).

Configuration for user management (read, update, delete) route handlers.

Passing an instance to `LitestarUsersConfig` will automatically take care of handler registration on the app.

Note:
- These routes make use of Litestar `Guard`s to require authorization. Callers require admin or similar privileges.

Passing an instance to `LitestarUsersConfig` will automatically take care of handler registration on the app.

classes.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig

methods.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.__post_init__
documentation:
Validate the configuration.

- A session backend must be configured if `auth_backend_class` is `SessionAuth`.
- At least one route handler must be configured.
- `role_model`, `role_create_dto`, `role_read_dto` and `role_update_dto` are required fields if
    `role_management_handler_config` is configured.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.auth_backend_class
documentation: The authentication backend to use by Litestar.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.secret
documentation: Secret string for securely signing tokens.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.user_model
documentation: A subclass of a `User` ORM model.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.user_service_class
documentation: A subclass of [BaseUserService][litestar_users.service.BaseUserService].

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.user_registration_dto
documentation: DTO class user for user registration.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.user_read_dto
documentation: A `User` model based SQLAlchemy DTO class.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.user_update_dto
documentation: A `User` model based SQLAlchemy DTO class.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.user_repository_class
documentation: The user repository class to use.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.authentication_request_schema
documentation:
The schema to use for authentication requests.

This can be a dataclass, pydantic `BaseModel` or msgspec `Struct`.
Requires an attribute with the same name as `user_auth_identifier` as well as a `password` attribute.

Notes:
    - Required if `user_auth_identifier` is set to a non-default value.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.auth_exclude_paths
documentation: Paths to be excluded from authentication checks.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.auto_commit_transactions
documentation: Whether to auto_commit transactions. Defaults to `False`.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.hash_schemes
documentation:
Schemes to use for password encryption.

Defaults to `["argon2"]`

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.session_backend_config
documentation:
Optional backend configuration for session based authentication.

Notes:
    - Required if `auth_backend_class` is `SessionAuth`.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.default_token_expiration
documentation: The default expiration time for authentication tokens.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.role_model
documentation:
A `Role` ORM model.

Notes:
    - Required if `role_management_handler_config` is set.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.role_create_dto
documentation:
A `SQLAlchemyDTO` based on a `Role` ORM model.

Notes:
    - Required if `role_management_handler_config` is set.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.role_read_dto
documentation:
A `SQLAlchemyDTO` based on a `Role` ORM model.

Notes:
    - Required if `role_management_handler_config` is set.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.role_update_dto
documentation:
A `SQLAlchemyDTO` based on a `Role` ORM model.

Notes:
    - Required if `role_management_handler_config` is set.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.user_auth_identifier
documentation:
The identifying attribute to use during user authentication. Defaults to `'email'`.

Changing this value requires setting `authentication_request_schema` as well, which would allow login via e.g. `username` instead.

Notes:
    - The attribute must be present on the `User` database model and must have a unique value.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.require_verification_on_registration
documentation: Whether the registration of a new user requires verification. Defaults to `True`.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.auth_handler_config
documentation:
Optional instance of [AuthHandlerConfig][litestar_users.config.AuthHandlerConfig]. If set, registers the route
handler(s) on the app.

Notes:
    - At least one route handler config must be set.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.current_user_handler_config
documentation:
Optional current-user route handler configuration. If set, registers the route handler(s) on the app.

Notes:
    - At least one route handler config must be set.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.password_reset_handler_config
documentation:
Optional password reset route handler configuration. If set, registers the route handler(s) on the app.

Notes:
    - At least one route handler config must be set.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.register_handler_config
documentation:
Optional registration/signup route handler configuration. If set, registers the route handler(s) on the app.

Notes:
    - At least one route handler config must be set.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.role_management_handler_config
documentation:
Optional role management route handler configuration. If set, registers the route handler(s) on the app.

Notes:
    - At least one route handler config must be set.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.user_management_handler_config
documentation:
Optional user management route handler configuration. If set, registers the route handler(s) on the app.

Notes:
    - At least one route handler config must be set.

class_variables.name: litestar_users.config.LitestarUsersConfig.verification_handler_config
documentation:
Optional user verification route handler configuration. If set, registers the route handler(s) on the app.

Notes:
    - At least one route handler config must be set.
Configuration class for LitestarUsers.

functions.name: litestar_users.route_handlers.get_registration_handler
documentation:
Get registration route handlers.

Args:
    path: The path for the router.
    user_registration_dto: A subclass of [UserCreateDTO][litestar_users.schema.UserCreateDTO]
    user_read_dto: A subclass of [UserReadDTO][litestar_users.schema.UserReadDTO]
    tags: A list of string tags to append to the schema of the route handler.

functions.name: litestar_users.route_handlers.get_verification_handler
documentation:
Get verification route handlers.

Args:
    path: The path for the router.
    user_read_dto: A subclass of [UserReadDTO][litestar_users.schema.UserReadDTO]
    tags: A list of string tags to append to the schema of the route handler.

functions.name: litestar_users.route_handlers.get_auth_handler
documentation:
Get authentication/login route handlers.

Args:
    login_path: The path for the login router.
    logout_path: The path for the logout router.
    user_read_dto: A subclass of [UserReadDTO][litestar_users.schema.UserReadDTO]
    auth_backend: A Litestar authentication backend.
    authentication_schema: The object that defines the request body schema.
    tags: A list of string tags to append to the schema of the route handlers.

functions.name: litestar_users.route_handlers.get_current_user_handler
documentation:
Get current-user route handlers.

Args:
    path: The path for the router.
    user_read_dto: A subclass of [UserReadDTO][litestar_users.schema.UserReadDTO]
    user_update_dto: A subclass of [UserUpdateDTO][litestar_users.schema.UserUpdateDTO]
    tags: A list of string tags to append to the schema of the route handlers.

functions.name: litestar_users.route_handlers.get_password_reset_handler
documentation:
Get forgot-password and reset-password route handlers.

Args:
    forgot_path: The path for the forgot-password router.
    reset_path: The path for the reset-password router.
    tags: A list of string tags to append to the schema of the route handlers.

functions.name: litestar_users.route_handlers.get_user_management_handler
documentation:
Get user management route handlers.

Note:
    Routes are guarded by role authorization.

Args:
    path_prefix: The path prefix for the routers.
    guards: List of Guard callables to determine who is authorized to manage users.
    identifier_uri: The path specifying the user ID and its type.
    opt: Optional route handler 'opts' to provide additional context to Guards.
    user_read_dto: A subclass of [UserReadDTO][litestar_users.schema.UserReadDTO]
    user_update_dto: A subclass of [UserUpdateDTO][litestar_users.schema.UserUpdateDTO]
    tags: A list of string tags to append to the schema of the route handlers.

functions.name: litestar_users.route_handlers.get_role_management_handler
documentation:
Get role management route handlers.

Note:
    Routes are guarded by role authorization.

Args:
    path_prefix: The path prefix for the routers.
    assign_role_path: The path for the role assignment router.
    revoke_role_path: The path for the role revokement router.
    guards: List of Guard callables to determine who is authorized to manage roles.
    identifier_uri: The path specifying the role ID and its type.
    opt: Optional route handler 'opts' to provide additional context to Guards.
    role_create_dto: A subclass of [RoleCreateDTO][litestar_users.schema.RoleCreateDTO]
    role_read_dto: A subclass of [RoleReadDTO][litestar_users.schema.RoleReadDTO]
    role_update_dto: A subclass of [RoleUpdateDTO][litestar_users.schema.RoleUpdateDTO]
    user_read_dto: A subclass of [UserReadDTO][litestar_users.schema.UserReadDTO]
    tags: A list of string tags to append to the schema of the route handlers.
